faqs.q: How quickly could a modular extension be installed at my Edgware home?
faqs.a: Once your design is finalised and any approvals are in place, we build your extension at our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks. Because the structure arrives largely complete, on-site installation in Edgware typically takes under a week — a far cry from the months of scaffolding and skips a traditional build brings. Most of the noisy, messy work happens 200 miles away, not in your garden.

faqs.q: Do I need planning permission for an extension in Edgware?
faqs.a: Not always. Permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, and many Edgware projects fall within them. The key local detail is knowing which council you sit under — most of Edgware is in Barnet, but streets west of the A5 fall within Harrow — so always check with your council before committing. We help you confirm what applies to your address as part of the design stage.

faqs.q: Will delivery and installation work on a typical Edgware street?
faqs.a: In almost all cases, yes. Edgware's interwar semis usually come with driveways and reasonable street widths, which makes craning or manoeuvring modules into position straightforward. We carry out a site survey early on to check access, overhead lines and parking arrangements, and we coordinate delivery timings so disruption to your neighbours is kept to a day or two rather than a season.

faqs.q: What do Edgware homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: Open-plan kitchen-diners are the most requested — the natural upgrade for a 1930s semi with a compact rear kitchen. We also see strong demand for home offices for Northern line commuters who now work part of the week from home, playrooms for growing families, and flexible ground-floor rooms that give an older parent or adult child comfortable space of their own.

heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Made for Edgware
intro:
Edgware's streets of 1930s semis were built for a different way of living — a compact kitchen at the back, a formal dining room at the front, and a garden that most families barely touch in winter. If your home feels a size too small but you love the area, the schools and the Northern line on your doorstep, extending almost always makes more sense than moving.

That's where EazyBase comes in. We design and factory-build modular home extensions in Edgware homeowners can actually plan their lives around: precision-engineered at our Blackburn facility in as little as four weeks, then installed on site in under a week. No months of mud, skips and uncertainty — just a warm, beautifully finished new room where your garden used to end.

Whether you're after an open-plan kitchen-diner, a quiet home office, a playroom or space for family who've moved back in, we handle design, build and installation as one seamless process, with a fixed scope you understand from day one.

localAngles.heading: Extending a 1930s Edgware semi
localAngles.body: Most of Edgware grew up in the 1920s and 30s, after the Underground arrived and turned farmland into family suburbs. The result is a housing stock we know inside out: bay-fronted semis with solid bones, decent plots and rear gardens with room to give. A single-storey modular extension across the back transforms that cramped original kitchen into a bright, open kitchen-diner — the room these houses were always missing. Because our modules are engineered to precise dimensions in the factory, they sit neatly against period brickwork rather than fighting it.

localAngles.heading: Planning across the Barnet–Harrow boundary
localAngles.body: Edgware sits right on the line between two boroughs — broadly, the A5 divides Barnet to the east from Harrow to the west — so the first question is simply which council your street falls under. The good news: permitted development rights often cover single-storey rear extensions, so many Edgware projects don't need a full planning application at all. Always confirm with Barnet or Harrow before you commit, though. We'll help you establish exactly what applies to your address, and prepare drawings if an application is needed.

localAngles.heading: Room for every generation
localAngles.body: Edgware is a family suburb in the fullest sense — it's common for grandparents, parents and children to share one address, whether by choice or by circumstance. A ground-floor extension is often the kindest answer: a comfortable, self-contained day room for an older parent, a snug for teenagers, or simply a bigger kitchen where everyone can eat together. Many local homeowners pair a rear extension with a loft conversion, adding bedrooms upstairs while our module delivers the living space below — two projects, one bigger home.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works in Edgware
localAngles.body: A traditional extension means months of trades, deliveries and noise on a quiet suburban street. Our approach moves nearly all of that to our Blackburn factory, where your extension is built under cover, unaffected by weather, in as little as four weeks. It then arrives in Edgware substantially complete and is installed in under a week. For homes near the Northern line where you're working from home, or on streets where parking is precious, keeping disruption to days rather than months matters as much as the finished result.

metaDescription: Factory-built modular home extensions for Edgware's 1930s semis. Built in 4 weeks, installed in under a week. Kitchens, offices, garden rooms and more.
